description: Halcyon Credit multi-scheme synthetic policy rulebook (US-203). Covers all six supported loan schemes. Every clause carries a stable clause_id (used as its source_id in retrieval and explanations) and, where the rule is machine-evaluable, a structured `rule` object consumed by the policy evaluation engine (US-206). Rules are synthetic and for POC use only.

rule_metric_glossary.dti: Debt-to-Income = amt_annuity / amt_income_total (annualised, both figures annual).
rule_metric_glossary.employment_months: abs(days_employed)/30.44; the HC2018 positive sentinel (>=0, e.g. 365243) means unemployed/pensioner => 0 months.
rule_metric_glossary.thin_file: True when employment_months < 24 or the employment sentinel is present.

clauses.title: Personal Loan Debt-to-Income (DTI) Threshold
clauses.text: Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio, calculated as the applicant's loan annuity divided by income, must not exceed 50% for Personal Loan approval. Applications with a DTI ratio above this threshold cannot be auto-approved and require referral to a human underwriter or an outright decline based on compensating factors.

clauses.title: Personal Loan Minimum Income Requirement
clauses.text: Applicants for a Personal Loan must demonstrate a minimum verified annual income of $15,000 (or local currency equivalent). Applications where verified income falls below this minimum income floor cannot be approved and must be referred for manual underwriting review.

clauses.title: Personal Loan Loan-to-Income (LTI) Cap
clauses.text: The total requested credit amount must not exceed 6 times the applicant's verified annual income (a Loan-to-Income, or LTI, cap of 6.0x) for Personal Loan products. Requests exceeding this cap require additional collateral, a co-signer, or explicit human underwriter override before approval.

clauses.title: Personal Loan Thin-File Escalation Rule
clauses.text: Applicants with fewer than 24 months of employment history, no prior credit bureau record, or an unemployment/pension sentinel value in the employment-duration field are classified as thin-file. Thin-file Personal Loan applications must be escalated to a human underwriter for manual review regardless of the model-predicted risk score or band.

clauses.title: Fairness Guardrail (All Schemes)
clauses.text: No recommendation may be based on, or materially influenced by, an applicant's gender, age, marital status, or other protected characteristic. Any model feature or policy rule found to correlate with a protected attribute above the fairness guardrail threshold must be excluded from automated decisioning and flagged for fairness audit before it can be used. This guardrail applies across every loan scheme.

clauses.title: Education Loan Affordability (DTI) Threshold
clauses.text: For Education Loan products, projected Debt-to-Income (DTI) after disbursement must not exceed 40%, reflecting that borrowers are frequently students with limited current earnings. Applications exceeding a 40% DTI must be referred for manual assessment of the co-applicant's capacity.

clauses.title: Education Loan Loan-to-Income (LTI) Allowance
clauses.text: Education Loans permit a higher Loan-to-Income allowance of up to 8.0x verified annual income (of the borrower or co-applicant), recognising the deferred-earnings profile of education financing. Requests above 8.0x LTI require a co-signer or manual underwriter override.

clauses.title: Education Loan Minimum Household Income
clauses.text: The verified household or co-applicant annual income supporting an Education Loan must be at least $12,000. Below this floor the application cannot be auto-approved and must be referred for manual review of repayment capacity.

clauses.title: Education Loan Thin-File Handling
clauses.text: A thin employment file is expected for Education Loan applicants and does not by itself require escalation; however, where the requested amount also exceeds a Loan-to-Income of 6.0x, the thin-file combination must be escalated to a human underwriter.

clauses.title: Vehicle Loan Debt-to-Income (DTI) Threshold
clauses.text: Vehicle Loan applications must maintain a Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio at or below 45%. Applications above this threshold require referral to a human underwriter given the depreciating nature of the financed asset.

clauses.title: Vehicle Loan Loan-to-Income (LTI) Cap
clauses.text: Because the financed vehicle is a depreciating asset, the Loan-to-Income cap for Vehicle Loans is 4.0x verified annual income. Requests exceeding 4.0x LTI must be declined unless a larger down-payment brings the financed amount within the cap.

clauses.title: Vehicle Loan Minimum Income Requirement
clauses.text: Applicants for a Vehicle Loan must demonstrate a minimum verified annual income of $18,000 to cover instalments, insurance, and running costs. Applications below this floor must be referred for manual review.

clauses.title: Business Loan Debt-Service (DTI) Threshold
clauses.text: Business Loan applications may carry a Debt-to-Income (debt-service) ratio of up to 55%, reflecting business cash-flow profiles, but any application above 55% must be referred for manual assessment of business financials.

clauses.title: Business Loan Minimum Trading History
clauses.text: The applicant business must show at least 24 months of trading or employment history in the relevant field. Where the employment/trading-duration field indicates fewer than 24 months or an unemployment sentinel, the Business Loan must be escalated to a human underwriter.

clauses.title: Business Loan Loan-to-Income (LTI) Cap
clauses.text: The requested Business Loan amount must not exceed 5.0x the verified annual income or declared business revenue. Requests above 5.0x LTI require security or a personal guarantee and must be referred for manual underwriting.

clauses.title: First-Time Buyer Conservative DTI Threshold
clauses.text: First-Time Buyer Loans apply a conservative Debt-to-Income (DTI) ceiling of 40% to protect borrowers new to credit. Applications exceeding 40% DTI cannot be auto-approved and must be referred to a human underwriter.

clauses.title: First-Time Buyer Loan-to-Income (LTI) Cap
clauses.text: First-Time Buyer Loans are capped at a Loan-to-Income of 4.5x verified annual income. Requests above this cap require a guarantor or additional deposit and must be referred for manual review.

clauses.title: First-Time Buyer Mandatory Manual Review
clauses.text: Because first-time borrowers typically present a thin credit file, every First-Time Buyer application with fewer than 24 months of employment history or no bureau record must be escalated for a mandatory human review before any recommendation is issued.

clauses.title: Low-Income Loan Reduced Income Floor
clauses.text: The Low-Income Loan scheme applies a reduced minimum verified annual income floor of $8,000 to widen access, while still requiring evidence of stable income. Applications below $8,000 must be referred for manual assessment of affordability and support options.

clauses.title: Low-Income Loan Strict Affordability (DTI) Cap
clauses.text: To protect low-income borrowers from over-indebtedness, the Low-Income Loan scheme enforces a strict Debt-to-Income (DTI) cap of 35%. Applications above 35% DTI must be declined or referred, as the higher affordability risk outweighs the inclusion benefit.

clauses.title: Low-Income Loan Loan-to-Income (LTI) Cap
clauses.text: Low-Income Loans are capped at a Loan-to-Income of 3.0x verified annual income to keep total borrowing proportionate to repayment capacity. Requests above 3.0x LTI must be referred for manual affordability review.
